design_philosophy.core_loop: Choose robot master stage → traverse platforming level with enemies → defeat boss → acquire special weapon → use weapon advantage to tackle other stages → confront Dr. Wily's factory
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Nonlinear stage select creates strategic decision-making: player must intuit weapon effectiveness matchups without explicit guidance, creating risk/reward around which boss to face first
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Transform from helper robot into combat-capable hero; gain tactical versatility through acquired powers; overcome increasingly difficult challenges through both skill execution and tactical preparation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game does not telegraph optimal stage order or weapon matchups—player must discover through experimentation or failure that certain Robot Masters are vulnerable to specific weapons. This creates a design philosophy where exploration of mechanic space is as important as execution skill. The arbitrary scoring system (removed in sequels) and limited weapon ammunition reinforce resource management tension alongside reflexes.

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with health gauge per stage; defeated enemies drop energy cells to replenish health
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-stage checkpoints; death returns to stage start. Players can revisit cleared Robot Master stages but not Wily stages once entered
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player chooses stage order and can farm weapons/energy by revisiting cleared stages. Difficulty scales based on weapon preparation—facing a Robot Master weak to an acquired weapon is substantially easier than facing it without preparation. This creates emergent difficulty curves based on player choice sequence

progression.upgrade_system: Special Weapon acquisition system: defeating each Robot Master adds their signature attack to Mega Man's arsenal with limited ammunition. Magnet Beam platform-generation item found in Elec Man stage. Each special weapon is more effective against specific Robot Masters, creating soft gating via advantage matchups rather than hard progression locks
progression.unlock_mechanism: Defeating Robot Masters unlocks their weapons. Completing all six Robot Master stages unlocks Dr. Wily's factory (7th stage) in stage select menu. Factory contains four linked stages that cannot be revisited
replayability_hooks: Scoring system encourages replays for high scores (though removed in sequels)
Different stage orders yield different difficulty curves and experiential variety
Weapon matchup discovery creates motivation to replay stages with new weapon combinations
Ability to revisit Robot Master stages to farm resources before Wily confrontation

world_building_techniques: Manual/instruction booklet plot: six Robot Masters created by Dr. Light, stolen and reprogrammed by Dr. Wily
Stage naming and visual theming (Cut Man's scissors motif, Fire Man's flame aesthetic, etc.) implicitly establish character identity without cutscenes
Ending sequence shows Mega Man returning home to Dr. Light after defeating Wily—minimal narrative resolution but thematic closure
Regional differences in narrative framing: Japanese version emphasizes Dr. Light/Wily rivalry; Western version emphasizes Wily's betrayal and theft of shared creations

boss_design.philosophy: Each Robot Master boss has a distinct attack pattern and movement logic tied thematically to their industrial purpose (Cut Man slashes, Fire Man launches fireballs, Guts Man creates earthquakes). Bosses are vulnerable to specific weapons, rewarding tactical preparation. Copy Robot in Wily Stage 2 mirrors player-chosen weapon progression. Final confrontation with Dr. Wily escalates through multiple phases
notable_bosses: Cut Man: slash attack pattern, weak to Rolling Cutter
Ice Man: ice-based projectiles, weak to Fire Storm
Bomb Man: lobbed bomb attacks, weak to Thunder Beam
Fire Man: flame column attacks, weak to Ice Slasher
Elec Man: electrical projectile attacks, weak to Rolling Cutter
Guts Man: earthquake/impact attacks, weak to Bomb
Copy Robot (Wily Stage 2): mirrors Elec Man's behavior; unused jump behavior suggests development iteration
Dr. Wily: multi-phase final boss encounter in Pacific-based robot factory

faction_indicators: Dr. Light faction (humanity-assisting robots, industrial purpose)
Dr. Wily faction (corrupted Robot Masters, factory-based empire ambitions)
danger_signals: Robot Masters turned hostile represent corruption of industrial automation
Wily's factory represents concentrated technological threat and centralized control risk
Mega Man's journey frames technological responsibility—Rock volunteers to become weapon after Dr. Light discovers threat, suggesting human agency over tool agency
environmental_storytelling: Each stage environment reflects Robot Master's function and aesthetic identity
Dr. Wily's factory scale and mechanical complexity visually escalate threat level
Ending scene returns focus to domestic/family context rather than combat arena, narratively centering human relationships over mechanical conflict
